title: Probe failures surface as one condensed warn/fail line with captured stderr — never raw duplicated CLI error dumps
action: Mode-gate record: 1 flag (cross-platform), 3 product files, small. install.sh probe_plugin_state: redirect each probe invocation's stderr into a temp file under STATE_TMP (one per CLI, e.g. codex-probe.err) instead of letting it stream to the terminal. In probe_broken_cli: repo-copy warn embeds the first line of that captured stderr in parentheses; plugin-first prints the captured stderr to the installer's own stderr, then fails with the existing named-CLI actionable message. install.ps1: honor the documented constraint at lines 104-106 (native stderr redirection under PS5.1 with ErrorActionPreference Stop becomes terminating NativeCommandError) — add a small helper that invokes the CLI's plugin list --json inside a scriptblock that sets ErrorActionPreference Continue and merges the error stream into the output stream, captures LASTEXITCODE, and separates ErrorRecord items (stderr lines) from plain strings (stdout). Use the helper at all four probe sites (codex pre-probe ~line 208, codex status ~214, claude pre-probe ~230, claude status ~236) so no raw red stderr ever prints; repo-copy failure produces a single Write-Warning with the first stderr line embedded; plugin-first failure Write-Hosts the captured stderr then Fails with the existing actionable message. Keep the file pure ASCII. e2e (bash): extend existing cases 16 and 16a — case 16 additionally asserts the fake stderr text (fake codex list forced failure) appears embedded in the output while exit stays 0; case 16a asserts the same diagnostic text appears in the failing output.

truths: a tolerated probe failure produces exactly one condensed warning line per broken CLI, with the CLI's first stderr line embedded (diagnostics preserved, noise removed)
plugin-first failure still surfaces the full captured stderr before the actionable refusal
install.ps1 gains no bare native stderr redirection outside the ErrorActionPreference-Continue scriptblock pattern
install.ps1 stays pure ASCII; all existing e2e cases stay green
prohibitions: do not drop the stderr content entirely — it must remain reachable in the output
do not restructure probe/transition/rollback flow beyond stderr handling
no edits outside the three files

trace.outcome: Probe stderr captured in both installers: repo-copy shows one condensed warning embedding the CLI's first stderr line; plugin-first surfaces captured stderr then refuses with the unchanged actionable message; ps1 uses EAP-Continue scriptblock (no bare native 2> redirect), pure ASCII verified
trace.verify_output: test_installers_e2e (bash): 21 passed, 0 failed — cases 16/16a strengthened to assert the captured stderr diagnostic (fake codex list forced failure) is embedded in the warn / surfaced before the plugin-first refusal. Orchestrator's own fresh run, exit 0.
